name: ION-14382 Booking JAR Size Blocker - Lambda Deployment Failure

entries.summary: Lambda deployment fails: Unzipped size must be smaller than 262144000 bytes (~250MB). JAR grew from ~94MB to ~135MB after AWS SDK v2 upgrade.
entries.detail: Build script deploys booking JAR as Lambda code for S3ArchiveLambda and ElasticsearchLambda. After AWS SDK v2 upgrade, unzipped JAR exceeds Lambda 250MB limit. Previous size was ~94MB, now ~135MB. Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Root cause: aws-crt native libs (+43.7MB), AWS SDK v2 services (+54.3MB), dual SDK v1+v2. Total unzipped 342MB vs 250MB limit.
entries.detail:
Root cause analysis complete. New JAR unzipped = 342MB vs Lambda limit 250MB. Old JAR = 230MB.

Size increase breakdown (112MB unzipped):
1. aws-crt native libs: +43.7MB (linux 35.7MB + osx 4.2MB + windows 3.8MB) — native JNI binaries for 7 platforms. NOT present in old JAR.
2. AWS SDK v2 services: +54.3MB unzipped — SSM(17.7MB), S3(11MB), DynamoDB(9MB), SESv2(8MB), SNS(3.6MB), SQS(2.8MB), SSO(1+1.3MB)
3. AWS SDK v2 core/http/regions: ~11MB  
4. Partially offset by removed SDK v1 services: DynamoDB(-), SSM(-), SES(-), DataPipeline(-) 
5. SDK v1 services STILL present via cloud-sdk-aws: S3, KMS, SNS, SQS (extended client uses v1)

Key targets for exclusion:
- aws-crt + aws-crt-client: -43.7MB unzipped (native libs for ALL platforms). Booking runs on x86_64 Linux only (Lambda + EC2). CRT is optional high-performance HTTP client — apache-client and netty-nio-client suffice.
- SSM (software.amazon.awssdk:ssm): -17.7MB if booking doesn't use SSM directly. Need to check if cloud-sdk-aws SSM is needed at Lambda runtime.
- SES v2 (software.amazon.awssdk:sesv2): -8MB if booking uses SNSClient not SES directly

If all 3 excluded: 342MB - 43.7MB - 17.7MB - 8MB = 272.6MB. Still over 250MB. Need deeper cuts.
Additional targets:
- SSO + SSO OIDC: -2.3MB (credential resolution, may be needed)
- S3 transfer manager can be excluded if not used directly
- netty-nio-client could be excluded if apache-client is the primary HTTP client
